purpose: A binary (or mixed-state) choice: a light-DOM element wrapping a real <input type="checkbox"> inside its own label, with an aria-hidden control span visualizing its state.
useWhen: An independent on/off choice submitted with a form ('I agree to the terms').
Selecting any number of items from a list — each checkbox stands alone.
A parent summarizing a group: `indeterminate` expresses 'some but not all'.

notWhen.case: Exactly one choice among mutually exclusive options.
The setting takes effect immediately, with no form submit.

guidance: `indeterminate` is visual and AT state only — it is never submitted, and any user toggle clears it (native behavior). Set it from the group's state, don't ask users to.
The invisible native input stretches across the whole label: hover, press, click, and focus land on it natively anywhere — never add click handlers to fake this.

default.description: The label content (the accessible name via the wrapping label).

checked.description: Indicator content shown while checked; replaces the drawn checkmark. Any markup: svg, icon-font glyph, HTML character, image.
guidance: The control is aria-hidden — the state is conveyed by the native input, so indicator content is purely visual.

unchecked.description: Indicator content shown while neither checked nor indeterminate.

indeterminate.description: Indicator content shown while indeterminate; replaces the drawn bar.
guidance: Each state slot only affects its own state — a customized checkmark leaves the drawn indeterminate bar in place unless this slot is also filled.

examples.title: Basic
examples.html: <zbk-checkbox name="terms" required>I agree to the terms</zbk-checkbox>

examples.title: Custom indicators
examples.html: Add to favorites★☆
examples.why: Indicator content layers over the aria-hidden control, sized by indicator-size and colored by indicator-color; the drawn checkmark is replaced only for the states provided.

examples.title: Indeterminate group parent
examples.why: Set indeterminate from the child checkboxes' mixed state; toggling the parent clears it natively.

examples.title: Labelless checkbox
examples.why: No accessible name. Even in dense table rows, give it label children (visually hidden if needed) or aria-label naming what it selects.
